hero.headline: JIIT Online MBA in Finance: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A | UGC-DEB Approved | Deemed University est. 2001 | Total fees ₹1,75,000

sections.tldr: JIIT Online MBA in Finance covers security analysis and portfolio management, financial statement analysis and business valuation, project appraisal and finance, options/futures and risk management, fixed income securities, behavioural finance, working capital management, and financial analytics. Mandatory Internship/OJT in Sem 3. AI in Business as core subject across all specs. Total fee ₹1,75,000 (course) + ₹6,000 exam + ₹1,000 registration = ₹1,82,000 all-in. Zero-cost EMI available. No scholarships. No bundled perks.

about.body:
JIIT's Online MBA in Finance has one of the deepest finance curricula in the sub-₹2L online MBA segment. Semester 3 covers six finance-specific subjects (security analysis, financial statement analysis, project appraisal, derivatives/risk management, fixed income, and behavioural finance) alongside mandatory Internship/OJT and an AI in Business core course. Semester 4 adds working capital management and financial analytics with a Major Project.

The inclusion of Behavioural Finance as a standalone subject is uncommon in online MBA programmes at this fee point. Most competitors cover behavioural finance as a module within a broader subject, not as a dedicated course. Fixed Income Securities as a standalone subject is also rare outside premium programmes.

Jaypee Institute of Information Technology (JIIT), Noida is a deemed university established in 2001. The institute's core identity is tech-adjacent, which shows in the mandatory AI in Business subject across all specialisations. NAAC A accredited and UGC-DEB approved.

At ₹1,75,000 course fee (₹1,82,000 all-in including exam and registration fees), the programme is mid-tier. No scholarships are offered. No bundled perks (Coursera, LinkedIn Learning, etc.).

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core (Economics, Financial Accounting, Marketing Management, OB, Business Statistics, Operations Management, Corporate Finance, Digital Transformation in Sem 1; Legal Aspects, HRM, Innovation and Entrepreneurship, Management Accounting, Business Research, Business Analytics, Digital Marketing, Capstone Project in Sem 2). Semesters 3-4 below show the Finance specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Artificial Intelligence in Business
subjects.desc: AI applications in business decision-making, ML fundamentals for managers, AI-driven automation, and ethical AI frameworks.

subjects.name: Internship/OJT
subjects.desc: Mandatory industry internship or on-the-job training. Applied learning in a real business environment with faculty supervision and evaluation.

subjects.name: Corporate Skill Development-2
subjects.desc: Professional skills workshop covering presentation, negotiation, business writing, and workplace communication at an advanced level.

subjects.name: Security Analysis and Portfolio Management
subjects.desc: Equity valuation techniques (DCF, PE, PBV), risk-return trade-off frameworks, Modern Portfolio Theory, CAPM, and portfolio construction for listed equities and bonds.

subjects.name: Financial Statement Analysis and Business Valuation
subjects.desc: Ratio analysis, DuPont decomposition, cash flow analysis, comparative financial analysis, and business valuation methods for M&A and investment decisions.

subjects.name: Project Appraisal and Finance
subjects.desc: Capital budgeting techniques (NPV, IRR, payback), project feasibility analysis, project financing structures, and infrastructure project appraisal frameworks.

subjects.name: Options, Futures and Risk Management
subjects.desc: Options pricing basics, futures contract mechanics, hedging strategies using derivatives, VaR calculation, and enterprise risk management frameworks.

subjects.name: Fixed Income Securities
subjects.desc: Bond valuation, yield curve analysis, duration and convexity, credit risk assessment, and fixed income portfolio construction.

subjects.name: Behavioral Finance
subjects.desc: Cognitive biases in investment decisions, prospect theory, herd behaviour, market anomalies, and behavioural portfolio theory.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Corporate strategy frameworks, competitive advantage analysis, strategic planning, diversification strategy, and strategic control.

subjects.name: Major Project
subjects.desc: Comprehensive finance project integrating Sem 1-4 learning. Industry-linked problem with faculty evaluation.

subjects.name: Working Capital Management
subjects.desc: Cash management, receivables management, inventory financing, short-term funding strategies, and working capital cycle optimisation.

subjects.name: Introduction to Financial Analytics
subjects.desc: Data-driven financial decision-making, financial modelling basics, Excel/Python for finance, and financial dashboard design.

whoHires.body:
JIIT MBA Finance graduates target roles across banking, corporate finance, and investment:

**Industries:** Commercial banking, NBFC, insurance, corporate treasury, equity research, fintech, financial consulting, and government finance departments

**Entry-level roles:** Financial Analyst, Credit Analyst, Equity Research Associate, Treasury Analyst, Risk Analyst, Banking Officer

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Finance Manager, Credit Manager, Portfolio Analyst, Treasury Manager, Financial Controller, Risk Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CFO (mid-market), VP Finance, Head of Treasury, Director of Credit Risk, Head of Corporate Finance

**Employers:** Banks (HDFC, ICICI, Axis, SBI), NBFCs (Bajaj Finance, Muthoot), insurance (LIC, HDFC Life), IT/ITES companies with finance functions (TCS, Infosys, Wipro), and NCR-region corporates.

**Note:** JIIT provides career support. The Jaypee brand is strongest in the NCR/North India engineering and technology sector. Online MBA brand recognition is developing.

skills.body:
The Finance specialisation builds skills across capital markets, corporate finance, and risk management:

**Technical skills:**
- Security Analysis: equity valuation (DCF, comparable), financial ratio analysis, DuPont decomposition, and earnings quality assessment
- Derivatives and Risk: options Greeks interpretation, futures hedging strategy design, VaR calculation, and enterprise risk register construction
- Fixed Income: bond pricing, yield spread analysis, duration matching, and credit risk scoring
- Project Finance: NPV/IRR calculation, project feasibility report preparation, and infrastructure financing structure analysis

**Applied skills:**
- Behavioural Finance: cognitive bias identification in investment committees, prospect theory application, and debiasing frameworks
- Financial Analytics: financial dashboard design, ratio trend automation, and data-driven credit assessment

**Soft skills:** Presenting investment analysis to committees, negotiating credit terms, managing audit relationships, and communicating financial risk to non-finance stakeholders.

comparisons.body:
Comparing JIIT Online MBA Finance against peer programmes:

**vs Amity Online MBA Finance:** Amity at approximately ₹2,25,000 with NIRF #22. JIIT at ₹1,82,000 all-in saves ₹43,000. Amity has a larger alumni network and broader spec variety. JIIT has 9 finance subjects in Sem 3 (vs typical 4-6), mandatory internship, and AI in Business as a core subject. Amity wins on brand recognition. JIIT wins on finance curriculum depth and fee.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Finance:** MUJ at approximately ₹1,80,000 with Manipal brand and Coursera Plus. JIIT at ₹1,82,000 is near-identical fee. MUJ has Coursera Plus bundled and stronger brand recognition. JIIT has mandatory internship, 9 Sem 3 subjects (vs MUJ's 6), and behavioural finance/fixed income as standalone courses. MUJ wins on brand and perks. JIIT wins on finance depth.

**vs IGNOU Online MBA Financial Management:** IGNOU at ₹66,000 is 2.8x cheaper with NAAC A++. IGNOU has no placement support, dated LMS, and no internship requirement. JIIT at ₹1,82,000 offers active LMS, mandatory internship, AI in Business, and deeper finance curriculum. IGNOU wins on cost and government recognition. JIIT wins on delivery and curriculum.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Finance:** NMIMS at approximately ₹1,96,000 has strong Mumbai BFSI recruiter connections and legacy brand. JIIT at ₹1,82,000 saves ₹14,000 with mandatory internship and AI integration. NMIMS wins on brand for Mumbai-centric BFSI careers. JIIT wins on fee and Sem 3 depth.

fees.body:
The JIIT Online MBA in Finance has a total all-in cost of ₹1,82,000.

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹44,500 (₹42,000 course + ₹1,500 exam + ₹1,000 one-time registration)
- Semester 2: ₹43,500 (₹42,000 course + ₹1,500 exam)
- Semester 3: ₹43,500 (₹42,000 course + ₹1,500 exam)
- Semester 4: ₹43,500 (₹42,000 course + ₹1,500 exam)
- **Total course fee: ₹1,68,000**
- **Total exam fee: ₹6,000**
- **Registration fee: ₹1,000**
- **Grand total: ₹1,75,000 + ₹6,000 + ₹1,000 = ₹1,82,000**

**Zero-cost EMI options:**
- Semester EMI: ₹7,417/month (6 EMIs per semester)
- Annual EMI: ₹8,800/month (10 EMIs per year)
- Full Course EMI: ₹9,723/month (18 EMIs for entire programme)

**Scholarships:** None offered.

**Bundled perks:** None (no Coursera, LinkedIn Learning, or third-party certifications included).

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Jaypee JIIT each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Finance as the specialisation, issued by Jaypee Institute of Information Technology (JIIT), Noida under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. It is valid for government recruitment requiring an MBA, PSU applications, and private-sector employers who verify UGC-DEB entitlement. WES recognised for international credential evaluation. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from JIIT Online MBA Finance students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Ankit S.
items.liked: 9 finance subjects in Sem 3 is genuinely deeper than any other online MBA I compared at this price point. Behavioural Finance and Fixed Income as standalone courses are rare
items.disliked: JIIT online MBA brand recognition is limited. Most people know Jaypee for BTech, not MBA. This matters during interviews when the recruiter checks university name
items.body: I work in a fintech startup in Noida. The derivatives, financial analytics, and project appraisal subjects were directly applicable. The mandatory internship in Sem 3 was genuinely useful for building portfolio evidence. At ₹1,82,000 all-in, the value is solid for the curriculum depth. The main limitation is brand recognition. Recruiters in NCR know JIIT for engineering but the online MBA is newer and less established.

items.name: Priya K.
items.liked: AI in Business as a core subject gave me vocabulary for fintech conversations that a purely traditional finance MBA would not cover
items.disliked: No scholarships and no bundled perks. At a similar fee, MUJ offers Coursera Plus free, which adds tangible value
items.body: I chose JIIT over MUJ because the finance curriculum is deeper. 9 Sem 3 subjects including behavioural finance and fixed income vs MUJ's 6. The trade-off is that MUJ has Coursera Plus and the Manipal brand. For my career in banking in NCR, the JIIT curriculum depth mattered more than the brand. The zero-cost EMI at ₹7,417/month made it manageable.

items.name: Suresh R.
items.liked: Working Capital Management in Sem 4 was practical and applicable to my manufacturing finance role
items.disliked: Career services for online MBA students could be more active. The on-campus placement infrastructure does not extend meaningfully to online learners
items.body: The curriculum is the strong point. The delivery is adequate but not exceptional. Some recorded lectures felt like campus recordings adapted for online. The mandatory internship was logistically challenging since I was already working full-time. I managed it by aligning it with a project at my company, but the programme could be clearer about how working professionals should handle this requirement.

faqs.question: Is the Internship/OJT in Sem 3 mandatory for working professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Internship/OJT is a credited component in Semester 3. Working professionals typically align it with a project at their current employer. The programme allows OJT (On-the-Job Training) as an alternative to a separate internship placement.

faqs.question: How does JIIT Finance compare to MUJ Finance at a similar fee?
faqs.answer: Near-identical fee (₹1,82,000 vs ₹1,80,000). JIIT has 9 finance Sem 3 subjects including behavioural finance and fixed income as standalone courses. MUJ has Coursera Plus bundled and the Manipal brand. Choose JIIT for finance depth. Choose MUJ for brand and perks.

faqs.question: Are there any scholarships or bundled perks?
faqs.answer: No. JIIT does not offer scholarships for the online MBA. No bundled perks (Coursera, LinkedIn Learning, etc.) are included. The fee is ₹1,82,000 all-in with zero-cost EMI options available.

faqs.question: What is the total all-in cost?
faqs.answer: ₹1,75,000 course fee + ₹6,000 exam fees (₹1,500/sem x 4) + ₹1,000 registration = ₹1,82,000 total. Zero-cost EMI: ₹7,417/month (6 EMIs), ₹8,800/month (10 EMIs), or ₹9,723/month (18 EMIs).

faqs.question: What is JIIT's NIRF rank?
faqs.answer: JIIT is unranked in the NIRF University category. It holds NIRF #83 in Engineering. The institute is NAAC A accredited, UGC-DEB approved, and WES recognised. Online MBA brand recognition is developing compared to flagship competitors.

chooseIf: You want the deepest finance curriculum in the sub-₹2L online MBA segment (9 Sem 3 subjects including behavioural finance and fixed income).
Mandatory internship/OJT adds experiential value to your MBA beyond pure classroom learning.
AI in Business as a core subject matters for fintech or tech-adjacent finance roles.
Zero-cost EMI from ₹7,417/month fits your monthly budget.
consider: Brand recognition matters for your employer. JIIT is known for BTech, not online MBA. MUJ or Amity have stronger MBA brand positioning.
MUJ at ₹1,80,000 with Coursera Plus offers similar fee with bundled perks and Manipal brand.
You need scholarships. JIIT offers none. Amity, MAHE, and others have scholarship categories.
Your target employer filters by NIRF rank. JIIT is unranked in University category.
